gpt-5.5.failure_modes_analysis: The generated scene includes the main labels and correct basic elements (image plane, O, p/P points, green projection ray, dashed future ray, red motion arrows). However, the initial rendered view is much more compact and frontal than the source: the image plane is small and nearly rectangular instead of dominating the left side as a large oblique parallelogram, and the rays/points are compressed with different apparent slopes and placements. Proportions between the plane, points, labels, and ray lengths are noticeably off, especially the plane size and the distance to P.
gpt-5.5.failure_modes_reasoning: Camera-Wrong applies because the initial viewpoint/orientation does not match the source figure’s oblique composition and ray directions. Scale-Wrong applies because the image plane and spatial distances are much smaller/compressed relative to the points and labels compared with the reference.
gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_analysis: The scene constructs a real 3D projection diagram: an image plane at focal distance, camera center O, world point P, projected point p, the projection ray O-P, 3D and image-plane motion arrows, and a dashed future ray are all present as separate 3D meshes/lines. The geometry is not a pasted texture or flat billboard; depth comes from the actual positions of O, the plane, P, and the camera view. Minor issues are that the image plane is slightly asymmetrically sized/positioned and the motion labels are placed at arrow endpoints rather than clearly on the vectors, but the topology and spatial relationships are correct.
gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_reasoning: All major elements are represented with plausible 3D geometry, correct intersections and connections, and only minor stylistic/proportional deviations.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_analysis: The code includes OrbitControls for drag/zoom, but this does not count as a meaningful interaction. It has a compact bottom-left Δt button that visibly toggles the motion construction on/off, including the dashed future ray, red velocity arrows, projected motion segment, and motion labels. It registers hover handlers on several scene elements that show a tooltip, change the cursor, and attempt to highlight the object; these should be visibly functional for most solid objects/lines. It also registers click handlers on the image plane, projection ray, points, and velocity arrows to show a small explanatory popup, plus Escape to dismiss it. The interactions are pedagogically useful and unobtrusive, with only a tiny button and small tooltip/popup that do not cover important geometry; however, there is no guided step-through demo or parameter slider.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_reasoning: There are at least two functional, pedagogically useful interactions—the Δt toggle and object hover/click explanations—with compact UI, but no guided demo, so this fits score 4 rather than 5.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_analysis: The rendered figure contains the same basic semantic elements (a plane, black points, green solid/dashed rays, red arrows, and p/P/O labels), but the first-frame composition is very different: the source is a wide, zoomed-in perspective with a large cropped plane on the left and the P point far to the right, while the render is a compact centered scene with the plane fully visible, much shorter rays, smaller scale, and a different camera angle. Label placement, point positions, line slopes, margins, and overall proportions do not match well enough for an inline replacement.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_reasoning: Although recognizable in concept, the crop, zoom, camera angle, spacing, and proportions differ substantially from the source, making it a <65% match.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_analysis: Source labels are O, p, ṗ, P, and Ṗ. In the generated figure, p, ṗ, P, and Ṗ are present, readable, and generally placed near the corresponding image/scene points, with roughly appropriate serif font size. The source label O is rendered as lowercase “o” and is placed to the right/below the left point rather than below-left as in the source. Overall the label set is mostly complete and clear, but one label has incorrect capitalization/text and somewhat off placement.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_reasoning: Four of five labels are correct and readable, while the O label is wrong as lowercase “o” and misplaced, fitting a minor 1-label issue under score 4.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_analysis: The figure illustrates perspective projection of a moving 3D point P from camera center O onto an image plane at focal distance f, with a 3D velocity Ṗ inducing an image-plane motion ṗ. The geometry and tooltips are largely figure-specific and correct, explaining the ray O–P, intersection at p, focal plane, and induced image motion; the main weakness is that the “instantaneous” velocity is represented as a finite step from P to PNext and p to pNext without giving the actual differential projection relationship.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_reasoning: The main concept is accurately conveyed with specific labels and interactions, but one minor conceptual simplification/missing detail remains in treating finite projected displacement as instantaneous ṗ.
